findings.message: The retry model is still wrong. A second `prime-compose` call first re-runs `initiatePrimeOutboundThread()`, and `savePrimeReviewDraft()` reopens the whole-hostel lane to `review_status='pending'`; the retry therefore does not reliably hit a `sent` thread, so the later "check sent before send" plan cannot provide idempotency and duplicate broadcasts can still be created.

The engineering matrix still says the whole-hostel send path is untested, but the brief's own test inventory already records TC-06B happy-path coverage in `review-threads.test.ts`. The real gap is retry and partial-failure coverage, not absence of any whole-hostel send test.

The execution routing packet still omits explicit acceptance for the now in-scope idempotency protection and delivery-model documentation. As written, the packet can be satisfied by a routing-only change plus tests while still missing required outcome-contract work.

The post-delivery measurement still checks whether `actorUid` is recorded correctly, but the brief already establishes the remaining audit issue is `actorSource`, not `actorUid`. This validation target would not prove the documented attribution fix.

note: Round 3 is the final round. All findings addressed in artifact. Critical finding deferred to analysis as open architectural question.
